pi_items.verified_value: PID.3 is Patient Identifier List; PID.5 is Patient Name - both are standard HL7 v2.x PID segment fields
pi_items.source: https://hl7-definition.caristix.com/v2/HL7v2.3/Fields/PID.3 and HL7 PID segment definition documentation

pi_items.verified_value: AA is Application Accept/Acknowledgment - standard HL7 MSA-1 code indicating successful message processing
AE is Application Error - standard HL7 MSA-1 code indicating soft errors in message processing
HL7 v2.x is the widely-used, real healthcare messaging standard for clinical data exchange

pi_items.metadata_value: MLLP framing with SB=0x0b, EB=0x1c, CR=0x0d
pi_items.verified_value: MLLP (Minimal Lower Layer Protocol) is the standard HL7 transport framing with start block (VT, 0x0b), end block (FS, 0x1c) + CR (0x0d)
ADT is Admission, Discharge, Transfer message type; A04 is Patient Registration event
MSA segment contains acknowledgement codes. MSA|AA for success, MSA|AE for application error are standard HL7 formats
NextGen Connect (formerly Mirth Connect) is a real, widely-used healthcare HL7 integration platform

privileged_info_summary: This task uses real HL7 v2.x standards: PID.3 (Patient Identifier) and PID.5 (Patient Name) are standard PID segment fields. Success acknowledgements use AA (Application Accept) and error acknowledgements use AE (Application Error), both standard MSA-1 codes. Messages use ADT^A04 (Patient Registration) event type. MLLP (Minimal Lower Layer Protocol) framing is the standard HL7 transport with 0x0b start marker and 0x1c 0x0d end markers. The task integrates with NextGen Connect, a real healthcare HL7 integration platform. All test data is synthetic (generated in the verifier script).
